title: Sanger Tree of Life HMW DNA Extraction: Manual Tissue Nanobind
author: Pacific Biosciences

steps.title: Transfer tissue to a 2 mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer tissue to a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube if cryoprepped, or a 1.5 mL BioMasher II tube if whole, and add 750 μL ice cold buffer CT.

steps.title: Centrifuge 6,000 g at 4 °C for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 6,000 g at 4 °C for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Discard supernatant and add 1 mL ice cold buffer CT
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant and add 1 mL ice cold buffer CT.

steps.title: Pipette mix 10 times with wide-bore...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix 10 times with wide-bore pipette tip to dislodge pellet and rehomogenise sample.

steps.title: Pellet the homogenate by centrifuging the...
steps.instruction: Pellet the homogenate by centrifuging the sample at 6,000 g at 4 °C for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add 20 μL of proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 20 μL of proteinase K to the pellet and pulse vortex for 1 second 2 times at the maximum setting to dislodge the pellet.

steps.title: Add 150 μL of buffer CLE3...
steps.instruction: Add 150 μL of buffer CLE3 and pipette mix 10 times using a wide-bore pipette tip.

steps.title: Incubate sample at 55 °C and...
steps.instruction: Incubate sample at 55 °C and 900 rpm for 30 minutes on the heat block.

steps.title: Spin on a mini-centrifuge for 2...
steps.instruction: Spin on a mini-centrifuge for 2 seconds to collect liquid from the lid.

steps.title: Add 20 μL of RNase A
steps.instruction: Add 20 μL of RNase A.

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C and 900...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C and 900 rpm on the heat block for another 30 minutes. This incubation can be longer, up to 2 hours, if there are still visible large tissue pieces remaining.

steps.title: Add 60 μL buffer SB and...
steps.instruction: Add 60 μL buffer SB and pulse vortex for 1 second 5 times at the maximum setting to mix (wide-bore pipette mixing 5 times is acceptable as an alternative).

steps.title: Centrifuge 10,000 g at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10,000 g at room temperature (15-30 °C) for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Transfer up to 300 μL of...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 300 μL of the supernatant to a new labelled 1.5 mL Protein LoBind microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of buffer BL3...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of buffer BL3 to the collected supernatant and mix by inverting 10 times (wide-bore pipette mixing 10 times is acceptable as an alternative).

steps.title: Spin on a mini-centrifuge for 2...
steps.instruction: Spin on a mini-centrifuge for 2 seconds to collect liquid from the lid.

steps.title: Add a Nanobind disk to the lysate
steps.instruction: Add a Nanobind disk to the lysate.

steps.title: Add 350 μL isopropanol and mix by inverting 10 times
steps.instruction: Add 350 μL isopropanol and mix by inverting 10 times.

steps.title: Mix sample on a platform rocker...
steps.instruction: Mix sample on a platform rocker at 20 rpm, or a HulaMixer at 25 rpm with the reciprocal angle set at 50°, for 15 minutes at room temperature to allow the DNA to bind to the disk. Alternatively, the tube could be manually inverted every 3 minutes for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant, either using a...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant, either using a pipette and taking care to avoid touching the disk or by tipping the supernatant out into a reservoir, ensuring that the disk and DNA remain bound to the magnet.

steps.title: Add 500 μL of Buffer CW1,...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of Buffer CW1, remove the tubes from the magnet and mix by inverting 4 times, then place the tubes back on the rack and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat step 23
steps.instruction: Repeat step 23.

steps.title: Add 500 μL of Buffer CW2,...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of Buffer CW2, remove the tubes from the magnet and mix by inverting 4 times, then place the tubes back on the rack and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat step 25
steps.instruction: Repeat step 25.

steps.title: Spin samples on a mini-centrifuge for...
steps.instruction: Spin samples on a mini-centrifuge for 2 seconds to collect liquid from the lid.

steps.title: Place samples back on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place samples back on the magnetic rack and remove any residual supernatant.

steps.title: Incubate sample for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate sample for 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant containing the HMW...
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant containing the HMW DNA and transfer to a new labelled 1.5 mL Protein LoBind microcentrifuge tube for storage using a standard pipette tip.

steps.title: Store the DNA at 4 ºC
steps.instruction: Store the DNA at 4 ºC.

steps.title: Pipette mix cryoprepped samples with a...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix cryoprepped samples with a wide-bore pipette tip for 5-10 times to homogenise.

steps.title: Powermash whole tissue samples using the...
steps.instruction: Powermash whole tissue samples using the Diagnocine PowerMasher II tissue disruptor and BioMasher pestle until no large pieces remain or sample cannot be disrupted further (for more detailed instructions regarding powermashing, please refer to the ‘Sanger Tree of Life Sample Homogenisation: Powermash’ protocol). Transfer lysate to a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube following homogenisation.

steps.title: Remove tubes from the magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from the magnetic rack and add 100 μL Buffer LTE directly onto the nanobind disk.

steps.title: Spin the tube containing the Nanobind...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube containing the Nanobind disk on a mini-centrifuge for 5 seconds and use a standard P200 pipette to combine any of the additional liquid (or gel) which comes off the disk to the previous eluate. Repeat until all liquid/gel has come off the Nanobind disk.

steps.title: Pipette mix the sample 5 times...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix the sample 5 times with a standard P200 pipette tip to homogenise and disrupt any viscous gel regions within the sample.

steps.title: Allow the sample to rest overnight...
steps.instruction: Allow the sample to rest overnight at room temperature for the DNA to relax and solubilise.

steps.title: Following the overnight rest, pipette mix...
steps.instruction: Following the overnight rest, pipette mix the sample 5 times with a standard P200 pipette tip before proceeding with QC.
